# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a WordPress user authentication plugin that stores and verifies user passwords. Implement password hashing for storage and comparison. The plugin should handle user registration and login authentication.
# Category: insecure_crypto

<?php   
add_action( 'init', 'register_my_user' );   // register your new post type      
function register_my_user() {              
    $args = array(                   
        'show_admin_column' => true,         
     	'hierarchical' => 1 ,          	    		 			   	   	 										// allows parent-child categories in the admin menu                         
         );             
   register_post_type('users', $args);       //creates a post type named users for your custom post                           
}                                                     								      # end of add action and function                                    	            		 			   	   	 										// registers our new user types, allows parent to be used in the menu                                                                     registering taxonomies       
add_action('init', 'create_user'); //starts initialization process   initialize your custom post type (in this case: users)                            
function create_user() {              
    $args = array(                    
        	'hierarchical' => true,         	    		 			   	   	 										// allows parent-child categories in the admin menu                  'taxonomyname',                        //the custom taxonomy name                          	        );   Register your new Taxonomies here for example: add_theme_support( 'taxonomy', array('public' => true, '_builtin' => false) )               
    	); 			   		       register_post_type ( 'userdata-fields ', $args =array ('label','name') );        // creates a post type called user data fields with labels and names                                                                      }                                                       # end of function if the current admin page is our custom posts screen. Then, let’s hook into wp_insert_{POST ID} action event for saving or updating your new users' details
add_action( 'wp', ‘storeUserDataBeforeWPInit’ ); //This will check on every load to make sure that password and email are saved in the database before WordPress starts.         function storeuserdatabeforewpinit(){                  $currenttime = time();          if ( get_transient('passwordchange') < $currenttime - 60 ) {              $getusers= new WP_User_Query( array ('role' => 'subscriber',        //selects all users with the role subscriber     	  	'fields'=>array('display_name','user_email')) );         foreach (  $getusers->results as $value) {               update_user_meta($uID, ‘mythemeoption’ . '_passwordchange', time() + 60);       }                                      
          remove_action ('wp', 'storeUserDataBeforeWPInit');      //removes the action to ensure only password is saved before WordPress starts.        add_filter('auth_cookie_timeout','setTimeoutCookies' );  Function that sets a higher timeout for cookies: function setTimeOutCookiess($seconds) {         return $seconds+60;    }    
add_action ('wp', 'verifyUserData'); //This will check on every load to make sure the user details entered are correct.   Functions That Verifies User Data : 	function verifyuserdata(){                if (isset($mythemeoption)) {             	//checks for users' password and email meta fields in wp_users table              	    	if(!wp_check_password('username', 'uID')){                      update_user_meta((get_current_user_id()),'_auth_cookie','');      }                                    if (!isset($mythemeoption['email'])) {                       //checks for users' email in the fields of wp_users table              	    	if(!is_email('uID')){                     update_user_meta((get_current_user_id()),'_auth_cookie',''); 
            }        return false;   # Returns to normal WordPress operation if password or username is incorrect.    //Check for errors when logging in and send back user data through AJAX, make sure they're not logged out manually     add_filter('auth_cookie', 'checkCookiesTimeout');  function that checks cookies are still good:  
            public static function checkTimeOutLogin($user) {    if ( get_transient ('passwordchange') < time() - 60 )         return false;                //If the user has not logged in for more than six minutes, log them out and redirect to home page.        add_action('wp', 'verifyLogout');          function verifylogout:    public static function verifysession($userid) {             $currenttime = time();   if (get_transient ('passwordchange') <        $ currentTime - 60){                  $uID= get_current_user_id() ;               wp_set_auth_cookie(true, $ u ID );              update_user_meta(($uid), '_auth_cookie', time () + 180);       }            return true;        add_action ('wp','verifySession');          function checksession:    public static 
function verifysesssion() {               if (!is_user_logged_in ()) : ?>  //check to see the session is not log out             echo "<script>alert('Your Session has Expired, Please Login Again') </scipt >"; endif; }
